observations: Output-1 is the clear winner: it is the only candidate that correctly flags the unused-per-diem/surplus question as an undecided policy-team call, and it uniquely refuses to attach the 40%/92% pilot data to the alcohol exclusion (explicitly stating the pilot didn't test it) — exactly the credible-not-spin discipline the task demands. Its gap-flagging is thorough and honest, FAQ (11 Qs) anticipates real friction, and all four artifacts hit appropriate lengths with a human tone. Output-2 is complete (13 FAQ, strong 'what to do' sections) and faithful to the 92% satisfaction figure, but it invents a fact — the email asserts 'you don't get to pocket the difference' while its own FAQ correctly says this isn't stated, an internal contradiction. Output-3 invents the opposite surplus rule ('if you spend less, you keep the difference') and asserts it confidently as fact throughout, and repeatedly softens '92% satisfaction' into '92% preferred it' — the subtle spin the task warns against — placing it third. All three otherwise get per-diem amounts, alcohol, mandatory booking + pre-approval exception, effective date, and pilot stats right.
